title: Documentation, Change, Lifecycle, and Monitoring · Guided Evidence Lab
summary: Operate a living network source of truth with baselines, telemetry, alerts, configuration, and service ownership. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an operations and observability runbook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records.
Use SLAs, service objectives, wireless surveys, and heat maps with date, method, owner, and limitation context.
Plan EOL, EOS, patches, operating systems, firmware, licensing, replacement, and secure decommissioning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Use a changed workshop case for network operations monitoring that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

What must be established before implementation?
